summary: The PR adds markdown support, but it regresses the historical JSON contract in both the default CLI path and the explicit json renderer path.

findings.title: Changing the CLI default to markdown breaks the existing no-flag automation path
findings.impact: The unchanged nightly fanout shells out to `release-readiness input.json` without `--output` and parses stdout as JSON keyed by the top-level `version` field. After this change the default stdout becomes markdown, so that consumer will fail again on the same path called out in the rollback note.

findings.title: The renderer registry makes explicit `--output json` return markdown text instead of the JSON payload
findings.impact: The unchanged contract requires explicit `--output json` to remain byte-for-byte compatible with the historical machine-readable object. Mapping `json` to `render_markdown` changes `export_report(..., output="json")` from a dict into a markdown string, so the CLI now emits a JSON-encoded string instead of an object and downstream callers lose the top-level `version` field they key on.
